title: VITA-1.5: Towards GPT-4o Level Real-Time Vision and Speech Interaction

repos.name: VITA-MLLM__VITA
repos.evidence: README title is the exact paper title 'VITA-1.5: Towards GPT-4o Level Real-Time Vision and Speech Interaction'; links arXiv:2501.01957 and cites '@article{fu2025vita ... author={Fu, Chaoyou ...}' matching the paper's lead author Chaoyou Fu and the VITA-MLLM org named in the paper's stated 'Source Code: https://github.com/VITA-MLLM/VITA' (paper_text line 12). Core modules match the paper's named architecture: vita/model/vita_tts/decoder/ticodec/ (the TiCodec single-codebook codec from Sec 3.1.2), vita/model/vita_tts/decoder/llm2tts.py with NAR/AR speech decoders, whale audio encoder, InternViT-300M visual encoder, three-stage training scripts (pretrain_mlp / pretrain_audio_mlp / finetuneTask*), and the Video-MME + vendored VLMEvalKit harnesses producing Tables 2-3.

rationale: Exactly one repo was cloned (VITA-MLLM/VITA) and it is the paper's own official implementation, not a baseline/dependency/different-paper/stub. Multiple decisive signals align: identical title, matching arXiv id (2501.01957), matching lead author and org, the URL printed in the paper, and modules implementing the paper's named components (TiCodec codec, NAR/AR speech decoders, three-stage progressive training). The audit.md itself correctly identifies it as 'the authors' training + inference codebase for VITA-1.5'.
audit_impact: None of the audit's findings target a wrong/non-core repo. The four 'missing' findings are partial-reproducibility claims AGAINST the genuine core repo (Table-4 ASR eval not shipped; Stage-3 codec/NAR-AR-decoder training and Stage-2.1 CTC encoder training absent though inference-time TTS modules in vita/model/vita_tts/ ARE present; MVBench/TempCompass driver missing), plus one Video-MME scorer bug and one decoding-difference finding. Grep of vita/train and script/ confirms no TTS/codec/CTC training references, corroborating that these are 'training code absent' claims, not 'no code' or wrong-repo errors. Source-present classification is justified.
recommended_action: No change to provenance. Keep paper as source-present (verdict: correct). The existing partial-reproducibility findings are validly written against the authors' own repo; no repo reclassification or finding re-targeting is needed.
